title: To ensure national uniformity with respect to certain requirements relating to preterm infant formula, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill is about special baby formula made for babies who are born too early. The government will study how available this formula is and what rules should apply to it. While this study happens, states cannot make their own new rules about this special formula for two years. However, if a company does something really wrong on purpose that hurts or kills a baby, families can still sue that company in court.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1  Preterm Infant Formula
sections.text:
The Secretary of Health and Human Services, working with the Food and Drug Administration, must do a study about special baby formula for premature babies. This study will look at how much of this formula is available in the United States. It will also examine all the federal and state laws and rules that affect this special formula. This includes laws about how the formula is designed, tested, made, sold, and used. The study will also look at whether companies should need government approval before selling this formula. Within two years after this law passes, the Secretary must send a report to Congress with the study results and recommendations.

For two years after this law passes, states cannot make their own rules about preterm infant formula that are different from or add to federal rules. This means states cannot create new requirements about how this formula is made, sold, or used during this time period. However, states can still take legal action against companies that intentionally do something wrong when making the formula that causes death or serious injury.

If a company is sued in state court and claims the state law doesn't apply because of this federal law, the company can move the case to federal court. People suing the company must prove by clear and convincing evidence that the company acted with willful misconduct. Any lawsuits that were already started before this law passes must be dismissed if they try to enforce state rules that this law now blocks.

Preterm infant formula means special baby formula for babies born before thirty seven weeks or babies with low birth weight. A manufacturer is a person or company that makes, packages, or labels this formula for sale, but not someone preparing it just for a baby in their direct care. Willful misconduct means doing something on purpose to cause harm, knowing it's wrong, and ignoring obvious risks where the harm is much more likely than any benefit.
